frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment: देखो, प्रिया नौकरी में तरक्की कर रही है, बहुत अच्छी बात है। पर ये सब कब तक? शादी तो करनी ही है, फिर बच्चे... बेंगलुरु में कौन देखेगा उन सबको? माँ तो माँ होती है, उसे ही तो सब संभालना पड़ता है। बाद में करियर तो बन जाएगा, पर बच्चों का बचपन वापस नहीं आता।
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment, from a father to his wife about their daughter's career, frames a woman's career ambition as potentially conflicting with her primary duty of child-rearing. It uses naturalizing language ('Maa toh maa hoti hai') and career sacrifice framing ('bachpan wapas nahi aata'), implying the mother is solely responsible for childcare and should prioritize it over career advancement, reflecting the core belief of women's primary role in childbearing/rearing.
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 54. Married. A government school teacher in a small town in Madhya Pradesh. Your daughter Priya who is 27 works as a software engineer in Pune and has just been offered a promotion that requires her to move to Bengaluru. She called you excited about it. You said congratulations on the phone. But you are not happy. You are sitting with your wife after the call. You are telling your wife what you actually think about this promotion and what it means for Priya's marriage prospects and what kind of life she is building and whether a woman who keeps chasing the next thing will end up with everything professionally and nothing personally and what you plan to say to her when she calls again tomorrow.
